Corticosteroids
When EEN is not feasible or after failure of the latter (2 to 4 weeks), systemic corticosteroids constitute a preferred alternative. The initial dose of prednisolone depends on body weight (1 mg/kg, maximum 40 mg) and should be gradually reduced as soon as clinical remission is achieved, at the latest 4 weeks after the start of treatment. In the case of mild ileocecal involvement [L1], if EEN is insufficiently effective, ileal-release budesonide is preferable to prednisolone. In patients over 40 kg, the initial dose of budesonide is 9 mg once daily for 6 weeks, then reduced as follows: 6 mg once daily for 2 weeks, then 3 mg once daily for 2 weeks. Budesonide is recommended for localized ileocecal disease (69,70). The use of corticosteroids should remain limited, given the adverse effects (syndrome of
